description_long: This apartment building is a mid-rise rectangular structure with five visible floors, each featuring a series of symmetrically arranged white-framed windows. The facade is characterized by alternating horizontal bands of dark and light materials, giving it a modern, utilitarian appearance. The ground floor includes a main entrance and a few larger window or door openings, while the upper floors are more uniform in window placement. The roof is flat and bordered by a low parapet, typical of urban residential buildings. The exterior appears to be finished with a combination of concrete, brick, and possibly some stone or stucco elements. There are no visible balconies or decorative architectural features, emphasizing a functional and straightforward design.
description: A five-story modern apartment building with alternating dark and light horizontal bands and symmetrically arranged white-framed windows. The structure has a flat roof and a simple, utilitarian facade.
description_view_0: This view shows the main facade with the entrance at the bottom left and a mix of window sizes, including a larger ground-floor opening.
description_view_1: This side displays a more uniform arrangement of windows and horizontal bands, with no visible entrance or large openings.
description_view_2: Similar to view 1, this side features evenly spaced windows and consistent horizontal banding, maintaining the building's symmetry.
description_view_3: This view highlights another facade with a cluster of windows on the lower right and a consistent window pattern above, but no visible entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in horizontal rows, with consistent spacing and alignment across all floors, creating a regular grid pattern.
